gameCodeGuidance.currentEnvironmentCommercialClaimCounter: Environment Commercial Excellence v1 Phase 1 defines environmentCommercialClaimMatrix as the machine-readable target claim taxonomy for commercial environment readiness. Every target claim keeps exactly one canonical packageCounter equal to the claim id in lower_snake_case, for example environment_commercial_ready, environment_vulkan_strict_aggregate_ready, environment_platform_windows_d3d12_ready, environment_asset_pipeline_openexr_ktx_basis_ready, environment_physical_weather_simulation_ready, and environment_artist_workflow_ready. Do not invent PascalCase, phase-numbered, backend-inferred, legacy, compatibility, or alias counters for these claims. A target claim can move to ready only when its validationRecipeIds exist, its dependsOn rows are ready where required by static checks, and its forbiddenInference text remains enforced. Adjacent non-claims stay in environmentCommercialUnsupportedAdjacentClaims and must not become package counters or unsupportedProductionGaps rows.

gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dMorphVisibleDeformation: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds emit vs_morph scene shader artifacts, fill D3D12/Vulkan scene renderer desc morph_vertex_shader and Win32DesktopPresentationSceneMorphMeshBinding rows, let SceneGpuBindingInjectingRenderer resolve explicit mesh-to-morph palette entries into MeshCommand::gpu_morphing, and require scene_gpu_morph_mesh_resolved, renderer_gpu_morph_draws, and renderer_morph_descriptor_binds on the D3D12 primary package path. This is the generated-package visible-deformation bridge first proven with POSITION deltas and extended by desktopRuntime3dMorphNormalTangentPackageSmoke for tangent-frame NORMAL/TANGENT payload and shader reads; compute morph package output rendering is tracked separately by desktopRuntime3dComputeMorphPackageSmoke, and this path does not add scene schema morph authoring, skin+morph composition, directional-shadow morph rendering, Vulkan/Metal parity, public RHI handles, or broad generated 3D production readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dMorphNormalTangentPackag: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds ship cooked/source morph_mesh_cpu bind NORMAL/TANGENT streams plus target NORMAL/TANGENT delta streams, align generated scene vertex input to kRuntimeSceneTangentSpaceStrideBytes, declare tangent semantics, and generate vs_morph shader source that reads morph POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT delta buffers through descriptor bindings 0/2/3 with weights at binding 1. This is D3D12-primary generated graphics morph package smoke only; compute morph package output rendering is tracked separately by desktopRuntime3dComputeMorphPackageSmoke, and it does not add scene schema morph authoring, skin+morph composition, directional-shadow morph rendering, Vulkan/Metal parity, public RHI handles, or broad generated 3D production readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dComputeMorphPackageSmoke: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds can pass packaged compute morph shader bytecode through Win32DesktopPresentationD3D12SceneRendererDesc::compute_morph_vertex_shader and compute_morph_shader or Win32DesktopPresentationVulkanSceneRendererDesc::compute_morph_vertex_shader and compute_morph_shader, select explicit compute_morph_mesh_bindings rows, dispatch host-owned create_runtime_morph_mesh_compute_binding work against cooked POSITION data, render the compute-written POSITION output through make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding, and require scene_gpu_compute_morph_mesh_bindings, scene_gpu_compute_morph_dispatches, scene_gpu_compute_morph_queue_waits, scene_gpu_compute_morph_mesh_resolved, and scene_gpu_compute_morph_draws in the D3D12 package smoke and toolchain-gated Vulkan package smoke. When --require-compute-morph-normal-tangent is requested, generated D3D12 and Vulkan scaffolds pass tangent-frame compute VS/CS bytecode, request output_normal_usage/output_tangent_usage, bind compute-written POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT vertex streams through the SDL3 scene renderer descriptor, and require scene_gpu_compute_morph_tangent_frame_output. Generated skin+compute package smoke is tracked separately by desktopRuntime3dComputeMorphSkinPackageSmoke and now has D3D12 plus toolchain-gated Vulkan POSITION-output package evidence. When --require-compute-morph-async-telemetry is requested, generated D3D12 package smoke reports first-party scene_gpu_compute_morph_async_compute_queue_submits, scene_gpu_compute_morph_async_graphics_queue_waits, scene_gpu_compute_morph_async_graphics_queue_submits, scene_gpu_compute_morph_async_last_compute_fence, scene_gpu_compute_morph_async_last_graphics_wait_fence, and scene_gpu_compute_morph_async_last_graphics_submit_fence from host-owned IRhiDevice::stats() only. Vulkan package evidence covers POSITION, NORMAL/TANGENT, and skin+compute through explicit SPIR-V artifacts; async compute overlap, Metal parity, directional-shadow morph rendering, scene-schema compute-morph authoring, public native handles, GPU timestamps, and broad renderer quality remain follow-up work.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dComputeMorphSkinPackageS: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds can ship a cooked skinned mesh row, expose --require-compute-morph-skin, generate D3D12 and toolchain-gated Vulkan vs_compute_morph_skinned/cs_compute_morph_skinned_position artifacts, switch the packaged scene mesh to the skinned mesh only for the requested smoke, pass Vulkan bytecode through Win32DesktopPresentationVulkanSceneRendererDesc::compute_morph_skinned_shader with explicit compute_morph_skinned_mesh_bindings rows, and require scene_gpu_compute_morph_skinned_mesh_bindings, scene_gpu_compute_morph_skinned_dispatches, scene_gpu_compute_morph_skinned_queue_waits, scene_gpu_compute_morph_skinned_mesh_resolved, scene_gpu_compute_morph_skinned_draws, scene_gpu_compute_morph_output_position_bytes, renderer_gpu_skinning_draws, and renderer_skinned_palette_descriptor_binds. This is host-owned generated D3D12 plus toolchain-gated Vulkan package evidence only; graphics morph+skin composition, async compute overlap/performance, Metal parity, directional-shadow morph rendering, scene-schema compute-morph authoring, public native handles, and broad skeletal animation readiness remain follow-up work.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dPackageStreamingSafePoin: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-package-streaming-safe-point after runtime scene validation loads the selected .geindex. The smoke calls mirakana::runtime::execute_selected_runtime_package_streaming_safe_point over the manifest packageStreamingResidencyTargets row, validates selected required preload asset hints, resident resource kind hints, and resident package count hints before commit, and reports package_streaming_status, package_streaming_ready, package_streaming_resident_bytes, package_streaming_resident_budget_bytes, package_streaming_committed_records, package_streaming_stale_handles, and package_streaming_diagnostics through first-party status fields. After a host has also uploaded selected cooked textures, mirakana::runtime_rhi::make_runtime_package_streaming_frame_graph_texture_bindings can validate the committed result plus live resident catalog rows and produce imported FrameGraphTextureBinding values for caller-owned Frame Graph execution without exposing IRhiDevice or native handles to gameplay. A host can also use mirakana::runtime_rhi::upload_runtime_package_streaming_frame_graph_texture_bindings with explicit caller-reviewed RuntimeTexturePayload rows to upload through upload_runtime_texture and receive RuntimeTextureUploadResult rows, imported bindings, uploaded-byte totals, and Frame Graph command-list plus transition counters in one host-owned transaction; when RuntimeTextureUploadOptions::upload_ring is set, that transaction can reuse a caller-owned RhiUploadRing path while keeping ownership host-side. For selected resident mesh, skinned mesh, and morph mesh CPU rows, a host can use mirakana::runtime_rhi::upload_runtime_package_streaming_mesh_gpu_bindings, mirakana::runtime_rhi::upload_runtime_package_streaming_skinned_mesh_gpu_bindings, and mirakana::runtime_rhi::upload_runtime_package_streaming_morph_mesh_gpu_bindings with explicit caller-reviewed payload rows to upload through the matching runtime upload helpers and receive runtime upload rows, renderer GPU binding rows, submitted fences, uploaded-byte totals, Frame Graph command-list counters, and upload_queue_waits_recorded evidence when async copy-queue uploads are consumed by graphics bindings; mirakana::runtime_rhi::wait_for_runtime_uploads_on_queue records those backend-neutral GPU-side waits without CPU waits or native handle exposure. Mesh/skinned/morph package transactions can reuse caller-owned RhiUploadRing paths through RuntimeMeshUploadOptions::upload_ring, RuntimeSkinnedMeshUploadOptions::upload_ring, and RuntimeMorphMeshUploadOptions::upload_ring while keeping ownership host-side; those rings may be backed by explicit RhiStagingBufferLease chunks through RhiUploadRingDesc::buffer. The committed generated 3D D3D12 package can require --require-package-upload-staging, which calls mirakana::runtime_rhi::execute_runtime_package_upload_staging_evidence, routes selected transactions through mirakana::runtime_rhi::make_runtime_package_resource_update_readiness, and reports package_upload_staging_* counters proving one texture, one static mesh, one skinned mesh, and one morph mesh package upload/binding transaction over four pool lease-backed rings with submitted fences, graphics waits for copy-queue uploads, and four async-ready resource update rows. This is selected host-gated safe-point execution plus narrow texture-binding/upload transaction, mesh/skinned/morph upload transactions, upload queue-wait evidence, and selected D3D12 package upload staging/resource-update smoke only; broad async/background package streaming, arbitrary eviction, texture streaming middleware, runtime-wide upload ring ownership beyond reviewed texture/buffer uploads, allocator/GPU budget enforcement, runtime-wide staging-pool ownership beyond explicit leases, renderer/RHI teardown execution, native handles, Metal readiness, async overlap/performance claims, and broad renderer quality remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dGpuSkinningPackageSmoke: sample_desktop_runtime_game can require --require-d3d12-gpu-skinning-evidence on the selected D3D12 installed package smoke to validate scene_gpu_skinned_mesh_bindings, scene_gpu_skinned_mesh_uploads, scene_gpu_skinned_mesh_resolved, renderer_gpu_skinning_draws, renderer_skinned_palette_descriptor_binds, d3d12_gpu_skinning_evidence_ready=1, and d3d12_gpu_skinning_evidence_selected=1 for the cooked skinned mesh scene. The separate installed-vulkan-scene-gpu-smoke recipe may require --require-vulkan-gpu-skinning-evidence and reports vulkan_gpu_skinning_evidence_ready=1, vulkan_gpu_skinning_evidence_selected=1, and the same GPU skinning counters without treating D3D12 proof as cross-backend evidence. This is selected package-visible GPU skinning evidence only; broad skeletal animation production, skin+morph composition, Metal parity, public native/RHI handles, and general renderer quality remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dFrameGraphMultiQueuePack: sample_desktop_runtime_game can require --require-framegraph-multiqueue-evidence on the selected D3D12 installed package smoke to run execute_frame_graph_rhi_multi_queue_package_evidence over the host-owned scene PBR RHI device and report framegraph_multiqueue_status=ready, framegraph_multiqueue_ready=1, framegraph_multiqueue_diagnostics=0, framegraph_multiqueue_command_lists_submitted=4, framegraph_multiqueue_queue_waits_recorded=3, framegraph_multiqueue_barriers_recorded=4, framegraph_multiqueue_aliasing_barriers_recorded=1, framegraph_multiqueue_pass_callbacks_invoked=4, framegraph_multiqueue_submitted_pass_fences=4, positive framegraph_multiqueue_copy_queue_submits, positive framegraph_multiqueue_graphics_queue_submits, positive framegraph_multiqueue_queue_waits, and framegraph_multiqueue_graphics_waited_for_copy=1, d3d12_framegraph_multiqueue_evidence_ready=1, and d3d12_framegraph_multiqueue_evidence_selected=1. This is backend-neutral multi-queue executor package evidence only; production graph adoption, async overlap/performance claims, native queue/fence/semaphore exposure, public RHI handles, Metal readiness, and broad renderer quality remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dRendererQualityPackageSm: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-renderer-quality-gates only on selected installed package smokes that already request scene GPU bindings and postprocess. The generated 3D descriptor reuses mirakana::evaluate_win32_desktop_presentation_quality_gate with scene GPU + postprocess requirements and expected framegraph_passes=2, then reports renderer_quality_status, renderer_quality_ready, renderer_quality_diagnostics, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_passes=2, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_render_passes=4, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_barrier_steps=4, renderer_quality_framegraph_passes_ok, renderer_quality_framegraph_render_passes_ok, renderer_quality_framegraph_barrier_steps_ok, renderer_quality_framegraph_execution_budget_ok, renderer_quality_scene_gpu_ready, and renderer_quality_postprocess_ready through first-party status fields. When --require-directional-shadow and --require-directional-shadow-filtering are selected on the separate generated shadow smoke, desktopRuntime3dDirectionalShadowPackageSmoke covers the framegraph_passes=3, framegraph_passes_executed=6, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=6, framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=15, and renderer_quality_directional_shadow_* gates. When --require-shadow-morph-composition is selected, desktopRuntime3dShadowMorphCompositionPackageSmoke covers the selected graphics morph + directional shadow receiver composition counters. gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dPostprocessDepthPackageS: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-postprocess-depth-input on selected installed package smokes that already request scene GPU bindings, postprocess, and renderer quality gates. The selected installed-d3d12-scene-gpu-smoke recipe may require --require-d3d12-postprocess-evidence and reports postprocess_d3d12_execution_status=ready, postprocess_d3d12_execution_ready=1, postprocess_d3d12_execution_selected=1, postprocess_d3d12_execution_shader_evidence_ready=1, and postprocess_d3d12_execution_passes_ok=1. The generated 3D D3D12/Vulkan scene renderer descriptors opt into enable_postprocess_depth_input, the package postprocess shader binds scene_depth_texture at t2 and scene_depth_sampler at s3, and status output reports postprocess_depth_input_ready=1 plus renderer_quality_postprocess_depth_input_ready=1, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_render_passes=4, renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_barrier_steps=9, framegraph_passes_executed=4, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=4, and framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=9 while keeping renderer_quality_expected_framegraph_passes=2. gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dDirectionalShadowPackage: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-directional-shadow and --require-directional-shadow-filtering on a separate selected installed package smoke that requests scene GPU bindings, postprocess, postprocess depth input, and renderer quality gates without morph, compute morph, or playable aggregate flags; the selected installed-d3d12-scene-gpu-smoke recipe may require --require-d3d12-shadow-cascade-policy and reports d3d12_shadow_cascade_policy_ready=1, d3d12_shadow_cascade_policy_selected=1, directional_shadow_cascade_count=4, directional_shadow_cascade_tile_width=225, directional_shadow_atlas_width=900, directional_shadow_atlas_height=225, directional_shadow_light_space_cascades=4, and directional_shadow_cascade_splits=5. gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dShadowMorphCompositionPa: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-shadow-morph-composition on a selected installed D3D12 package smoke that requests scene GPU bindings, morph package data, depth-aware postprocess, renderer quality gates, and fixed PCF directional shadow filtering without compute morph, skin composition, or playable aggregate flags. The generated 3D D3D12/Vulkan scene renderer descriptors keep graphics morph vertex shader and Win32DesktopPresentationSceneMorphMeshBinding rows active, compile shifted shadow receiver artifacts named shadow_receiver_shifted.ps.dxil and shadow_receiver_shifted.ps.spv with MK_SAMPLE_SHIFTED_SCENE_SHADOW_RECEIVER_PS, bind the shadow receiver descriptor set after the deformation descriptor set, and report scene_gpu_morph_mesh_bindings, scene_gpu_morph_mesh_uploads, scene_gpu_morph_mesh_resolved, scene_gpu_uploaded_morph_bytes, renderer_gpu_morph_draws, renderer_morph_descriptor_binds, directional_shadow_status=ready, directional_shadow_ready=1, fixed_pcf_3x3 filtering, framegraph_passes=3, framegraph_passes_executed=6, framegraph_render_passes_recorded=6, and framegraph_barrier_steps_executed=15 through first-party status fields. This is a narrow generated 3D graphics morph + directional shadow receiver package smoke; morph-deformed shadow-caster silhouettes, compute morph + shadow composition, graphics morph+skin composition, hardware comparison samplers, cascades, atlases, production shadow quality, Metal shadow presentation, GPU timestamps, backend-native stats, native handles, and broad renderer quality remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dNativeUiOverlayPackageSm: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-native-ui-overlay on a selected installed D3D12 package smoke that already requests scene GPU bindings, postprocess, renderer quality gates, playable_3d aggregate evidence, gameplay systems, and generated UI overlay DXIL shader artifacts. The generated 3D game builds a first-party MK_ui HUD box, submits it through MK_ui_renderer into the host-owned native UI overlay path, enables Win32DesktopPresentationD3d12SceneRendererDesc::enable_native_ui_overlay without texture atlas mode, and reports hud_boxes=2, ui_overlay_requested=1, ui_overlay_status=ready, ui_overlay_ready=1, ui_overlay_sprites_submitted=2, and ui_overlay_draws=2 through first-party status fields. This is a narrow generated 3D HUD box native UI overlay smoke; textured image-sprite atlas behavior is covered by desktopRuntime3dNativeUiTexturedSpriteAtlasPackageSmoke and text glyph atlas behavior is covered by desktopRuntime3dNativeUiTextGlyphAtlasPackageSmoke, while production text shaping, font rasterization, glyph atlas generation, runtime source image decoding, OS accessibility bridges, Metal overlay readiness, native handles, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain unsupported.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dEntityScaleCullingPackag: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-entity-scale-culling on a lightweight installed package smoke that already requests a cooked config and .geindex scene package. The smoke calls mirakana::runtime::plan_runtime_entity_scale_culling with deterministic 2D/3D entity rows and reports entity_scale_culling_status=planned, entity_scale_culling_ready=1, entity_scale_culling_rows=4, entity_scale_culling_visible_rows=2, entity_scale_culling_culled_rows=2, entity_scale_culling_lod_rows=2, entity_scale_culling_priority_update_rows=1, entity_scale_culling_normal_update_rows=2, entity_scale_culling_background_update_rows=1, positive entity_scale_culling_projected_draw_cost, positive entity_scale_culling_projected_update_cost, entity_scale_culling_budget_protected_rows=1, entity_scale_culling_diagnostics=0, and entity_scale_culling_budget_diagnostics=2 through first-party status fields without D3D12/Vulkan renderer selection, scene GPU bindings, or gameplay systems. This extends Entity Scale Culling Package Evidence v1 to generated 3D; it does not mutate scene nodes, perform GPU culling, expose native/RHI handles, or prove broad high-object-count performance.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dVisibleProductionPackage: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-visible-3d-production-proof on a selected installed D3D12 package smoke or --require-vulkan-visible-3d-production-proof on desktop-runtime-release-target-vulkan-visible-production-proof-toolchain-gated without treating either backend proof as cross-backend evidence. Both paths require scene GPU bindings, postprocess, renderer quality gates, playable_3d aggregate evidence, gameplay systems, native UI overlay, and target-specific UI overlay shader artifacts. The proof aggregates visible_3d_status=ready, visible_3d_ready=1, visible_3d_diagnostics=0, visible_3d_expected_frames=2, visible_3d_presented_frames=2, visible_3d_d3d12_selected=1 or visible_3d_vulkan_selected=1, visible_3d_null_fallback_used=0, visible_3d_scene_gpu_ready=1, visible_3d_postprocess_ready=1, visible_3d_renderer_quality_ready=1, visible_3d_playable_ready=1, and visible_3d_ui_overlay_ready=1 through first-party status fields. This is a narrow per-backend visible production-style aggregate; it is not Metal parity, native/RHI handle exposure, broad renderer quality, broad runtime UI readiness, or broad generated 3D production readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dNativeUiTexturedSpriteAt: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-native-ui-textured-sprite-atlas on a selected installed D3D12 package smoke that already requests scene GPU bindings, postprocess, renderer quality gates, playable_3d aggregate evidence, gameplay systems, generated UI overlay artifacts, and a cooked runtime GameEngine.UiAtlas.v1 asset. gameCodeGuidance.desktopRuntime3dPlayablePackageSmoke: DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and the committed sample can require --require-playable-3d-slice on selected installed package smokes that already request package streaming, scene GPU bindings, postprocess, and renderer quality gates. The smoke reports playable_3d_status, playable_3d_ready, playable_3d_diagnostics, playable_3d_expected_frames, playable_3d_scene_mesh_plan_ready, playable_3d_camera_controller_ready, playable_3d_animation_ready, playable_3d_morph_ready, playable_3d_quaternion_ready, playable_3d_package_streaming_ready, playable_3d_scene_gpu_ready, playable_3d_postprocess_ready, playable_3d_renderer_quality_ready, and selected compute readiness fields through first-party status output. gameCodeGuidance.currentRenderingBackendParityHostRecipeP: Backend Renderer Parity Host Recipe Proof v1 requires Metal BackendRendererParityProofRow entries to carry reviewed host_validation_recipe_id values from shader-toolchain, mobile-packaging, renderer-metal-apple-host-evidence, or ios-simulator-smoke. plan_backend_renderer_parity_policy reports missing_host_validation_recipe for omitted recipes and unreviewed_host_validation_recipe for arbitrary valid ids, validates recipe ids against native-token leakage, includes them in replay hashes, keeps Metal host-gated without Apple-host proof, and does not transfer D3D12 or Vulkan proof to Metal readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.runtimeMorphMeshGpuBinding: Narrow D3D12 primary-lane GPU morph proof: MK_runtime_rhi exposes upload_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu, make_runtime_morph_mesh_gpu_binding, and attach_morph_mesh_descriptor_set for RuntimeMorphMeshCpuPayload POSITION deltas at morph descriptor binding 0, optional NORMAL/TANGENT deltas at bindings 2/3, and 256-byte weight uniform binding 1; MK_renderer exposes MorphMeshGpuBinding normal_delta_buffer/tangent_delta_buffer metadata, MeshCommand::gpu_morphing, RhiFrameRendererDesc::morph_graphics_pipeline, gpu_morph_draws, and morph_descriptor_binds. Direct D3D12 readback proves graphics-pipeline POSITION deformation and NORMAL/TANGENT lit-output changes; RuntimeMorphMeshComputeBinding and create_runtime_morph_mesh_compute_binding now prove direct D3D12 compute POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT output readback, make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding proves POSITION renderer consumption, generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage D3D12/Vulkan smoke proves POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT compute output rendering plus package-visible queue-wait evidence when explicitly requested, make_runtime_compute_morph_skinned_mesh_gpu_binding plus SkinnedMeshGpuBinding::skin_attribute_vertex_buffer prove a D3D12 runtime/renderer skin+compute-morph composition path with a distinct joint-palette descriptor set, MK_runtime_scene_rhi retains RuntimeSceneComputeMorphSkinnedMeshBinding rows in SceneSkinnedGpuBindingPalette through a host-owned runtime scene RHI bridge, and generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage D3D12/Vulkan smoke can now require first-party skin+compute package counters while D3D12 also reports scene_gpu_compute_morph_async_* sequencing telemetry from IRhiDevice::stats(). RHI/D3D12 queue-submit and queue-wait fence sequencing telemetry is covered by runtime-rhi-compute-morph-async-telemetry-d3d12-v1 and generated package promotion is covered by generated-3d-compute-morph-async-telemetry-package-smoke-d3d12-v1. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Async Overlap Evidence D3D12 v1 adds mirakana::rhi::RhiAsyncOverlapReadinessDiagnostics and diagnose_compute_graphics_async_overlap_readiness, which deterministically reports not_proven_serial_dependency for the current same-frame graphics queue wait without exposing native handles, timestamp resources, or package-visible performance claims. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Pipelined Output Ring D3D12 v1 adds RuntimeMorphMeshComputeBindingOptions::output_slot_count, RuntimeMorphMeshComputeOutputSlot, slot-indexed make_runtime_compute_morph_output_mesh_gpu_binding, and D3D12 descriptor-range proof that slot 1 can receive compute-written POSITION output through binding 3 without aliasing slot 0. Runtime RHI Compute Morph Pipelined Scheduling D3D12 v1 adds mirakana::rhi::RhiPipelinedComputeGraphicsScheduleEvidence and diagnose_pipelined_compute_graphics_async_overlap_readiness so first-party diagnostics can classify graphics consuming a previous output slot while compute writes a different current slot as backend-private timing-ready rather than a same-frame serial dependency. RHI D3D12 Per-Queue Fence Synchronization v1 completed first-party FenceValue queue identity and backend-private per-queue D3D12 fence timelines. Vulkan async/performance work, Metal parity, graphics morph+skin composition, and broad generated 3D production readiness remain follow-up work; rhi-d3d12-queue-timestamp-measurement-foundation-v1 is the active RHI timing slice for backend-private graphics/compute timestamp interval diagnostics.
gameCodeGuidance.runtimeSceneRhiMorphGpuPalette: Host-owned package bridge for the GPU morph proof: MK_runtime_scene_rhi accepts RuntimeSceneGpuBindingOptions::morph_mesh_assets, validates selected cooked morph_mesh_cpu records, uploads them through MK_runtime_rhi including optional NORMAL/TANGENT delta streams when present, retains SceneMorphGpuBindingPalette plus morph_descriptor_set_layout and morph_mesh_uploads, reports morph_mesh_uploads/uploaded_morph_bytes, and releases morph buffers/descriptors through execute_runtime_scene_gpu_binding_safe_point_teardown on NullRhiDevice. Generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds can request/report host-owned palette counters, generated-package POSITION/NORMAL/TANGENT visible graphics morph smoke is tracked separately, and generated-package POSITION compute morph smoke is tracked by desktopRuntime3dComputeMorphPackageSmoke; the palette bridge itself does not add scene schema morph bindings, compute dispatch ownership, Vulkan/Metal parity, or gameplay-visible RHI handles.

gameCodeGuidance.currentMemoryDiagnostics: MK_core Memory Diagnostics v1 and Frame/Thread Scratch v1 provide dependency-free ScratchArena, ScratchLease, ScratchLeaseStatus, scratch_lease_status_label, MemoryLifetimeClass, MemoryCounterRow, MemoryDiagnosticsOptions, MemoryClassDiagnosticsSummary, MemoryDiagnosticsSummary, MemoryBudgetPressure, MemoryDiagnosticsCode, MemoryDiagnosticsStatus, summarize_memory_diagnostics, memory_lifetime_class_label, memory_budget_pressure_label, memory_diagnostics_code_label, and memory_diagnostics_status_label for bounded frame temporary / worker scratch leases and memory evidence by lifetime class. gameCodeGuidance.currentVulkanRuntimeOwners: Vulkan runtime owners now include VulkanRuntimeInstance, VulkanRuntimeDevice, VulkanRuntimeCommandPool, VulkanRuntimeBuffer, VulkanRuntimeTexture, VulkanRuntimeShaderModule, VulkanRuntimeDescriptorSetLayout, VulkanRuntimeDescriptorSet, VulkanRuntimePipelineLayout, VulkanRuntimeGraphicsPipeline, VulkanRuntimeComputePipeline, VulkanRuntimeSwapchain, VulkanRuntimeFrameSync, VulkanRuntimeReadbackBuffer, and VulkanRuntimeSampler. The create_rhi_device bridge exposes backend-neutral BufferHandle, TextureHandle, ShaderHandle, DescriptorSetLayoutHandle, DescriptorSetHandle, PipelineLayoutHandle, GraphicsPipelineHandle, ComputePipelineHandle, SwapchainHandle, SwapchainFrameHandle, graphics command lists, immediate FenceValue stats/waits, upload-buffer writes, descriptor updates for uniform/storage buffer, sampled texture, and sampler bindings, standalone texture transitions, buffer copies, buffer-to-texture copies, texture-to-buffer copies, standalone texture render-target clears, optional depth attachment clears, standalone texture render-target draws with optional depth attachments, copy-only submit, command-list-local texture state tracking, stale texture-state submit rejection, submit-success-only texture-state commits, and transient lease creation while keeping VkBuffer, VkImage, VkDeviceMemory, VkShaderModule, VkDescriptorSetLayout, VkDescriptorPool, VkDescriptorSet, VkWriteDescriptorSet, VkDescriptorBufferInfo, VkDescriptorImageInfo, VkSampler, VkBufferCopy, VkBufferImageCopy, VkPipelineLayout, VkPipeline, VkSurfaceKHR, VkSwapchainKHR, VkImageView, VkSemaphore, VkFence, and native commands private. Descriptor creation, update, and binding use create_runtime_descriptor_set_layout, create_runtime_descriptor_set, update_runtime_descriptor_set, descriptor-aware create_runtime_pipeline_layout, and record_runtime_descriptor_set_binding while VkDescriptorSetLayout, VkDescriptorPool, VkDescriptorSet, VkWriteDescriptorSet, VkDescriptorBufferInfo, vkUpdateDescriptorSets, vkCmdBindDescriptorSets, and Vulkan function pointers remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Pipeline creation uses create_runtime_pipeline_layout, create_runtime_graphics_pipeline, and create_runtime_compute_pipeline with dynamic rendering metadata, native DepthStencilStateDesc mapping, and compute shader modules while VkPipelineLayout, VkPipeline, VkPipelineVertexInputStateCreateInfo, VkGraphicsPipelineCreateInfo, VkComputePipelineCreateInfo, vertex input descriptions, and Vulkan function pointers remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Swapchain creation uses create_runtime_swapchain while VkSurfaceKHR, VkSwapchainKHR, VkImage, VkImageView, and image-view destruction order remain private to MK_rhi_vulkan. Frame sync, acquire, and present use create_runtime_frame_sync, acquire_next_runtime_swapchain_image, and present_runtime_swapchain_image while VkSemaphore, VkFence, VkPresentInfoKHR, vkAcquireNextImageKHR/vkQueuePresentKHR results, and acquired/presented image indices remain private or first-party mapped; the IRhiDevice bridge records shader-free swapchain clear render passes, queues present, submits through private synchronization2, and completes frame ownership on submit. Dynamic rendering color/depth clear and draw recording use record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_clear, record_runtime_dynamic_rendering_draw, record_runtime_texture_rendering_clear, and record_runtime_texture_rendering_draw while VkRenderingInfo, VkRenderingAttachmentInfo including pDepthAttachment, VkCommandBuffer, VkPipeline, VkImageView, viewport/scissor state, vertex/index buffers, and vkCmdBeginRendering/vkCmdBindPipeline/vkCmdBindVertexBuffers/vkCmdBindIndexBuffer/vkCmdDraw/vkCmdDrawIndexed/vkCmdEndRendering function pointers remain private. Standalone texture transition/copy recording uses build_texture_transition_barrier, record_runtime_texture_barrier, record_runtime_buffer_copy, record_runtime_buffer_texture_copy, and record_runtime_texture_buffer_copy while shader-read hazards, depth_write hazards, D24S8 depth|stencil image-barrier aspects, usage/format/state compatibility checks, VkImageMemoryBarrier2, VkDependencyInfo, VkBufferCopy, VkBufferImageCopy, vkCmdCopyBuffer, vkCmdCopyBufferToImage, vkCmdCopyImageToBuffer, and image layouts remain private or first-party mapped. Synchronization2 recording uses record_runtime_swapchain_frame_barrier and submit_runtime_command_buffer while VkDependencyInfo, VkImageMemoryBarrier2, VkSubmitInfo2, VkSemaphoreSubmitInfo, VkCommandBufferSubmitInfo, queue handles, semaphores, fences, and raw VkResult values remain private or first-party mapped; requested queue-idle waits must succeed before submit_runtime_command_buffer reports submitted, and logical device creation requires the synchronization2 feature before those commands are requested. Upload and readback use write_runtime_buffer, read_runtime_buffer, create_runtime_readback_buffer, record_runtime_swapchain_image_readback, and read_runtime_readback_buffer while VkBuffer, VkDeviceMemory, VkMemoryRequirements, VkPhysicalDeviceMemoryProperties, VkBufferImageCopy, mapped pointers, and native memory commands remain private or first-party mapped. Environment-gated visible draw/readback, raw texture-sampling, depth-ordered draw/readback, sampled-depth readback, height-fog runtime readback, physical-sky runtime readback, shadow receiver readback, RhiFrameRenderer runtime material texture-sampling, and cooked runtime scene GPU palette proofs now run through IRhiDevice when MK_VULKAN_TEST_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_TEXTURE_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_DEPTH_SAMPLE_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_POSTPROCESS_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_HEIGHT_FOG_DEPTH_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_HEIGHT_FOG_DEPTH_FRAGMENT_SPV plus MK_VULKAN_TEST_HEIGHT_FOG_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_HEIGHT_FOG_FRAGMENT_SPV and VK_LAYER_KHRONOS_validation, MK_VULKAN_TEST_PHYSICAL_SKY_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_PHYSICAL_SKY_FRAGMENT_SPV plus VK_LAYER_KHRONOS_validation, MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_SHADOW_RECEIVER_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_MATERIAL_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_RENDER_FRAGMENT_SPV, MK_VULKAN_TEST_COMPUTE_MORPH_TANGENT_FRAME_SPV, or MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_VERTEX_SPV/MK_VULKAN_TEST_RUNTIME_SCENE_FRAGMENT_SPV point at real SPIR-V artifacts and the local Vulkan runtime is available; default generated-game availability remains gated by runtime and shader-toolchain readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentMetal: SDK-independent MK_rhi_metal scaffold exposes backend identity, host support, capability profile, probe plan, deterministic probe result helpers, non-empty metallib shader library artifact validation, runtime/default-device/command-queue/shader-library readiness planning, texture synchronization planning for render/blit/present encoder intent, platform availability diagnostics, MetalEnvironmentFeatureHostEvidenceDesc, MetalEnvironmentFeatureEvidenceRequirement with synchronization_evidence_required, MetalEnvironmentFeatureEvidenceRow, MetalEnvironmentFeatureHostEvidencePlan, default_environment_feature_evidence_requirements, build_environment_feature_host_evidence_plan, and metal_environment_feature_host_evidence_status_line for feature-local physical_sky, height_fog, cloud_layer, precipitation, volumetric_fog, volumetric_cloud, and environment_lighting_ibl Apple host-gated evidence rows tied to renderer-metal-apple-host-evidence without promoting Metal readiness from Windows/D3D12/Vulkan proof or exposing native handles. Apple-only native paths expose MetalNativeEnvironmentFeatureHostEvidenceDesc, MetalNativeEnvironmentFeatureHostEvidenceResult, and create_native_environment_feature_host_evidence behind private Objective-C++ implementation; the reviewed recipe builds environment_feature_evidence.metallib from MSL, loads it as an MTLLibrary, creates render and compute pipeline states, creates cube HDR and depth/render target textures plus a particle buffer, executes command buffers, proves render and compute readback_nonzero, emits metal_environment_* counters, and keeps native_handle_access=0. The same Apple-only layer continues default-device, command-queue, command-buffer, render-encoder, texture-target, CAMetalLayer drawable acquisition, drawable render-encoder creation, drawable present scheduling, command-buffer submission, and texture-readback ownership through retained private PIMPL objects without including Metal or QuartzCore headers or exposing native handles. Apple Objective-C++ paths assign GameEngine.RHI.Metal.* GPU debugger labels through -[MTLObject setLabel:] on the default MTLDevice, MTLCommandQueue, per-allocation MTLCommandBuffer, offscreen and drawable-backed MTLTexture targets, MTLRenderCommandEncoder, and transient readback MTLCommandBuffer/MTLBlitCommandEncoder/MTLBuffer objects.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInput: VirtualInput for keyboard-style state, VirtualPointerInput with PointerSample/PointerState for mouse, touch, and pen-style pointer state, VirtualGamepadInput with GamepadButton/GamepadAxis state for gamepad buttons and axes, mobile touch sample mapping into first-party PointerSample values without native handles, VirtualPointerInput::touch_gesture for active touch count, centroid, centroid delta, and two-touch pinch scale primitives, TouchGestureRecognizer for tap, double-tap, long-press, pan, swipe, pinch, rotate, and canceled gesture events, and MK_runtime RuntimeInputActionMap plus RuntimeInputStateView and RuntimeInputContextStack for deterministic GameEngine.RuntimeInputActions.v4 keyboard, pointer, gamepad button, scalar key-pair, and scalar gamepad-axis action documents evaluated over first-party virtual input state through bind_key, bind_pointer, bind_gamepad_button, bind_key_axis, bind_gamepad_axis, bind_key_in_context, bind_pointer_in_context, bind_gamepad_button_in_context, bind_key_axis_in_context, bind_gamepad_axis_in_context, context-aware action_down, and context-aware axis_value. Use RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action to turn the next allowed first-party key, pointer, or gamepad-button press into a validated RuntimeInputRebindingProfile action override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_axis to capture the first deterministic gamepad axis magnitude past a deadzone into a validated axis override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action_with_focus when a host-owned menu has armed one capture row and needs deterministic focus_retained plus gameplay_input_consumed output for waiting, captured, or pressed-but-rejected candidates. Use RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationModel rows from make_runtime_input_rebinding_presentation when a runtime/game menu needs reviewed base/profile action and axis display labels plus symbolic glyph lookup keys. Empty context stacks use default; non-empty stacks select the first context that declares the requested action. Scalar action axes apply deterministic clamp/deadzone handling; broad UI focus integration, global input consumption/bubbling beyond focused rebinding capture, radial stick deadzones, response curves, pointer delta axes, gesture-to-RuntimeInputActions binding, full runtime/game rebinding panels outside the reviewed editor action capture lane, real platform input glyph generation/assets/rendering, keyboard-layout localization, keyboard key-pair axis capture, multiplayer device assignment, per-device profiles, SDL3, and native device handles remain follow-up adapter work.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInputContextPlanning: Use RuntimeInputContextStackRequest, RuntimeInputContextLayerDesc, RuntimeInputContextStackPlan, and plan_runtime_input_context_stack before evaluating RuntimeInputActionMap when generated games combine gameplay, menu, dialogue, rebinding, capture, or passive overlay layers. sample_2d_playable_foundation is the source-tree adoption reference for a passive HUD overlay plus gameplay stack. The planner validates context names, rejects duplicate contexts, supports default-context fallback, blocks lower-priority layers for modal rows, and reports gameplay_input_available, gameplay_input_consumed, ui_context_active, and capture_context_active without native handles, platform adapters, game-specific menu schemas, or a full UI focus/navigation system.
gameCodeGuidance.currentInputRebindingProfiles: MK_runtime RuntimeInputRebindingProfile and GameEngine.RuntimeInputRebindingProfile.v1 provide deterministic persisted full-row action and axis overlays over a caller-supplied base RuntimeInputActionMap. Use validate_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, apply_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, serialize_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, deserialize_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, load_runtime_input_rebinding_profile, and write_runtime_input_rebinding_profile through IFileSystem/RootedFileSystem. Use RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action for host-independent digital action trigger capture from first-party keyboard, pointer, and gamepad-button state into a validated action override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingAxisCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_axis for host-independent gamepad-axis and two-held-keyboard key-pair axis capture (capture_keyboard_key_pair_axes) from first-party VirtualGamepadInput axis magnitudes and VirtualInput key-down pairs into a validated axis override candidate. Use RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureRequest, RuntimeInputRebindingFocusCaptureResult, and capture_runtime_input_rebinding_action_with_focus for an armed/focused/modal capture guard that reports focus_retained while waiting and gameplay_input_consumed for waiting, captured, or pressed-but-rejected digital candidates after the guard passes. Use RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationToken, RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationRow, RuntimeInputRebindingPresentationModel, present_runtime_input_action_trigger, present_runtime_input_axis_source, and make_runtime_input_rebinding_presentation for host-independent display rows with deterministic labels and symbolic glyph lookup keys after loading, validating, or applying a profile. Visible full interactive runtime/game rebinding panels outside the reviewed editor action capture lane, broad UI focus integration, global input consumption/bubbling beyond focused rebinding capture, radial stick deadzones, response curves, pointer delta axes, gesture-to-RuntimeInputActions binding, real platform input glyph generation/assets/rendering, keyboard-layout localization, keyboard key-pair axis capture, multiplayer device assignment, per-device profiles, removed SDL3 input APIs, editor-private runtime APIs, native device handles, and cloud/binary saves remain follow-up adapter work.
gameCodeGuidance.currentProcessExecution: ProcessCommand, RecordingProcessRunner, run_process_command, RootedFileSystem, and Windows Win32ProcessRunner for shell-free executable-plus-arguments process execution with stdout/stderr capture and rooted project file access
gameCodeGuidance.currentToolExecution: ShaderToolProcessRunner and ShaderToolExecutionPolicy bridge shader compile commands to platform process execution with artifact output-root policy and reviewed executable override for PATH names, project-relative tool paths, or absolute installed SDK/tool paths; ShaderArtifactValidationProcessRunner bridges spirv-val validation commands to the same shell-free process policy; DXC Vulkan SPIR-V command planning uses -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3 through executable-plus-argument vectors; MK_editor_core make_viewport_shader_compile_requests queues default D3D12 DXIL and Vulkan SPIR-V viewport shader artifacts from one HLSL source, while make_material_preview_shader_compile_requests queues factor-only and base-color-textured material preview D3D12 DXIL/Vulkan SPIR-V artifacts from a material-preview HLSL source; discover_shader_tools finds deterministic dxc, spirv-val, metal, and metallib descriptors and tracks dxc.spirv-codegen capability markers for deterministic toolchain roots; evaluate_shader_toolchain_readiness reports D3D12 DXIL, Vulkan SPIR-V, DXC SPIR-V CodeGen, and Metal library readiness; editor ProjectShaderToolSettings executable values may be reviewed PATH tool names, project-relative tool paths, or absolute installed SDK/tool paths while working directory, artifact root, and cache index paths stay project-relative; make_spirv_shader_validation_command and execute_shader_artifact_validation_action provide reviewed SPIR-V artifact validation before native Vulkan shader module promotion; MK_editor_core ShaderToolDiscoveryState exposes discovered tools and readiness diagnostics to editor UI/AI workflows; pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1 reports local shader tool blockers, reads Process/User/Machine environment state, searches MK_SHADER_TOOLCHAIN_ROOT, User/Machine PATH, Vulkan SDK variables, and vcpkg tool roots before Windows SDK fallback, runs a real quoted DXC SPIR-V CodeGen probe, and tools/check-shader-toolchain.ps1 -RequireAll can gate CI lanes; MK_editor_core ViewportShaderArtifactState verifies non-empty compiled D3D12 VS/PS DXIL bytecode and Vulkan VS/PS SPIR-V artifacts before native viewport promotion and can query readiness by shader id for material preview variants; build_shader_artifact_provenance records compiler, command, artifact, and input fingerprints for cache invalidation; ShaderArtifactCacheIndex and execute_shader_compile_action provide persistent shader cache index storage, cache-hit reuse, .geprovenance writes, deterministic test artifact marker writes, and real artifact enforcement for compiler-backed runs; build_shader_hot_reload_plan and build_shader_pipeline_recreation_plan turn stale provenance or successful non-cached compiles into explicit pipeline recreation decisions; build_shader_pipeline_cache_plan batches selected compile requests into deterministic hot-reload, compile-required, pipeline-recreation, cache-entry-current, and cache-index-repair rows; reconcile_shader_pipeline_cache_index repairs selected first-party cache indexes from current provenance without invoking compilers or writing artifacts; execute_asset_import_plan runs asset import or recook plans through IFileSystem, decodes first-party texture/mesh/audio/material source documents including optional hex byte payloads, optionally routes PNG, glTF, and common audio sources through MK_tools-owned asset-importers adapters when MK_ENABLE_ASSET_IMPORTERS is enabled, writes deterministic cooked artifacts preserving first-party byte payloads, decoded PNG RGBA8 payloads, decoded common-audio PCM payloads, and glTF triangle POSITION-only or POSITION/NORMAL/TEXCOORD_0/TANGENT interleaved mesh byte payloads for embedded/loaded buffers while rejecting partial normal/UV/tangent-frame primitives; execute_asset_runtime_recook builds recook plans, executes imports transactionally, stages successful cooked artifacts through AssetRuntimeReplacementState, and rolls every requested asset back on recook transaction failure; execute_asset_runtime_package_hot_reload_replacement_safe_point runs reviewed recook execution, passes helper-owned staged rows into commit_runtime_package_hot_reload_recook_replacement_v2, and commits only selected package matched AssetRuntimeReplacementState rows after resident replacement success; scan_asset_files_for_hot_reload turns registered asset files into deterministic hot reload snapshots; PollingFileWatcher turns IFileSystem roots into deterministic added, modified, and removed file events for AssetHotReloadRecookScheduler inputs; WindowsFileWatcher turns Win32 API ReadDirectoryChangesW notifications into FileWatchEvent records; LinuxFileWatcher turns inotify notifications into FileWatchEvent records; MacOSFileWatcher turns FSEvents notifications into FileWatchEvent records; native watcher handles stay backend-private and host-unavailable backends report explicit diagnostics
gameCodeGuidance.currentAssets: AssetRegistry, AssetDependencyGraph, AssetHotReloadTracker, AssetHotReloadRecookScheduler for debounce-based source and dependency-invalidated recook requests, AssetHotReloadApplyState for failed-reload rollback state, AssetRuntimeReplacementState for staging runtime asset replacements until a safe point, committing only selected staged assets, and rolling back failed recooks without losing the active asset, AssetCookedPackageIndex with content hashes, dependency edges, serialization, and deterministic recook decisions, deterministic GameEngine.Tilemap.v1 metadata through TilemapMetadataDocument with AssetKind::tilemap and tilemap_texture package edges while native GPU sprite batching, package-authored atlas metadata workflows beyond the reviewed decode/pack path, and production atlas editor UX remain follow-up, ShaderMetadataRegistry with descriptor reflection rows, shader compile command planning including DXC Vulkan SPIR-V commands with -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3, shader tool runner validation, compiler output capture contracts, shader include dependency discovery, shader include dependency graph edge generation, shader artifact manifest serialization including reflection rows, AssetImportMetadataRegistry, first-party texture/mesh/audio source document serialization with optional hex byte payloads, build_asset_import_plan, build_asset_recook_plan, MaterialDefinition, MaterialInstanceDefinition, deterministic material/material-instance serialization, material instance composition, fixed material factor uniform sizing, and explicit MaterialPipelineBindingMetadata for backend-neutral descriptor/pipeline layout derivation with uniform-buffer, sampled-texture, and sampler rows; optional PNG/glTF/common-audio source import adapters are implemented in MK_tools behind the asset-importers feature and feed this first-party cooked-artifact path; MK_tools preserves first-party texture data_hex, mesh vertex/index data_hex, and audio data_hex into deterministic cooked artifacts, decodes PNG RGBA8 payloads into texture data_hex, decodes common-audio PCM samples into audio data_hex, and cooks glTF triangle POSITION-only or POSITION/NORMAL/TEXCOORD_0/TANGENT interleaved accessors plus indexed/generated indices into deterministic mesh.vertex_data_hex and uint32 mesh.index_data_hex for embedded/loaded buffers; MK_runtime now reads cooked package indexes through IFileSystem, validates runtime payload hashes and dependencies, exposes first-party runtime asset handles, provides typed cooked texture/mesh/audio/material/scene/tilemap payload access including decoded optional byte payloads, serialized scene text, tilemap atlas texture path checks, and tilemap_texture edge inspection, resolves scene material dependencies through resolve_runtime_scene_materials, stages all-or-nothing package replacement at safe points, owns host-independent save/settings/localization/input action session documents, and exposes RuntimeDiagnosticReport helpers for package load, payload, scene, and session failures; MK_runtime_rhi creates backend-neutral RHI texture resources, mesh vertex/index buffer upload paths with derived position-only stride 12 or lit tangent-space stride 48 metadata, 256-byte-backed material factor uniform buffers with 64-byte factor payloads and one-pass Frame Graph command-list evidence, sampler-aware owner-device-validated material descriptor bindings, and submitted_fence rows from typed runtime payload uploads; MK_runtime_scene_rhi turns cooked package scene packets into retained SceneGpuBindingPalette resources through those typed runtime payload parsers and RHI upload helpers, preserves actual submit-order submitted_upload_fences rows, and reports submitted_upload_fence_count plus last_submitted_upload_fence rows; MK_editor_core can turn selected cooked material/texture artifacts into a non-throwing GPU binding preview plan using typed runtime payload parsers; and renderer MeshCommand bindings can now consume those RHI handles for descriptor binding, vertex/index binding, and indexed draws through SceneGpuBindingPalette

MK_assets provides authored GameEngine.AnimationTransformBindingSource.v1 binding source rows through AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument for mapping scalar curve targets to named transform components. MK_tools can derive those rows from glTF node TRS targets through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_binding_source using glTF node names or deterministic fallback names, and can import full unit-quaternion 3D node transform tracks through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_tracks_3d and bridge them into cooked quaternion clip source rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_quaternion_clip. MK_assets exposes GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource.v1 and GameEngine.CookedAnimationQuaternionClip.v1 for selected 3D TRS tracks, and MK_runtime exposes runtime_animation_quaternion_clip_payload for cooked package records. MK_runtime_scene resolves scalar binding rows for RuntimeSceneInstance data through resolve_runtime_scene_animation_transform_bindings, can apply caller-provided FloatAnimationCurveSample rows through apply_runtime_scene_animation_transform_samples, and can apply validated AnimationPose3d rows to named RuntimeSceneInstance nodes through apply_runtime_scene_animation_pose_3d. MK_scene_renderer can sample a cooked scalar AnimationFloatClipSourceDocument and apply AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance data through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_float_clip, then rebuild the render packet for generated 3D package smoke validation. MK_scene_renderer can also sample AnimationJointTrack3dDesc rows, apply named-joint AnimationPose3d rows to RuntimeSceneRenderInstance scene nodes, and rebuild the render packet through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_pose_3d. MK_scene_renderer can also sample cooked scalar morph-weight clips against RuntimeMorphMeshCpuPayload rows through sample_runtime_morph_mesh_cpu_animation_float_clip and return deterministic CPU-morphed positions for generated 3D package smoke validation. Generated DesktopRuntime3DPackage scaffolds and sample_desktop_runtime_game can load cooked animation_quaternion_clip records through runtime_animation_quaternion_clip_payload, decode them with make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes, and validate --require-quaternion-animation through sample_and_apply_runtime_scene_render_animation_pose_3d plus quaternion_animation_scene_applied/quaternion_animation_scene_rotation_z counters. JointLocalTransform remains the Z-only local pose contract for existing clips, while AnimationJointLocalTransform3d covers translation, positive scale, and unit-quaternion local rotations for 3D model-pose construction; AnimationJointTrack3dDesc can sample selected translation, quaternion rotation, and positive-scale tracks into AnimationPose3d with rest-pose fallback for missing components, and AnimationJointTrack3dByteSource plus make_animation_joint_tracks_3d_from_f32_bytes decode cooked little-endian component byte rows into those descriptors. MK_tools can import LINEAR glTF node TRS 3D rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_tracks_3d with full unit-quaternion rotation keyframes for caller-mapped AnimationPose3d workflows and can emit GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource.v1 rows through import_gltf_node_transform_animation_quaternion_clip. Backward root-motion accumulation, negative-time wrapping, inferred clip duration, tangent-space material/render integration beyond the cooked tangent-frame schema, broader GPU skinning parity, broader IK constraints beyond the implemented planar segment local Z and 3D swing/twist local clamps, authored IK assets, and animation graph authoring are not implemented yet.
gameCodeGuidance.currentAudio: AudioMixer with logical buses, voices, asset-id clip references, clip metadata registration, deterministic mix plans, spatial_mix_plan for listener/source distance attenuation and stereo pan metadata, AudioStreamingQueue for decoded chunk append/consume/starvation state, sample-frame render scheduling, streaming buffer availability, device format conversion intent, underrun diagnostics, AudioClipSampleData validation, selectable nearest/linear resampling quality, and render_interleaved_float output with gain, channel conversion, and clamped interleaved float PCM. AudioGameplayMixRequest, AudioGameplayMixPlan, and plan_gameplay_audio_mix provide the fail-closed game-facing cue/bus/trigger planner: game-owned music, sfx, ambience, ui, voice, and custom cue rows can describe clip ids, buses, gain, looping, pause/mute/fade, trigger start frames, gain scales, and optional spatial metadata before converting into mixer-ready AudioBusDesc and AudioVoiceDesc commands without native handles or codec assumptions. AudioDeviceStreamRequest, AudioDeviceStreamPlan, plan_audio_device_stream, and render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float provide the device-independent stream pump baseline: host adapters report current queued frames and a target, MK_audio schedules bounded render chunks at device_frame + queued_frames, returns no_work when the queue is already full enough, and rejects invalid formats, invalid queue/render budgets, invalid resampling quality, and device-frame overflow. AudioProductionReviewRequest, AudioProductionReadinessPlan, and review_audio_production_readiness provide the selected production audio evidence gate for decoded source, streaming chunk, conversion, DSP graph, spatial/listener, HRTF host-gate, device lifecycle, unsupported claim, side-effect, diagnostic, selected-package, production-readiness, and replay-hash rows. Selected 2D/3D package samples can require --require-audio-gameplay-mixer and load cooked package audio payloads before reporting audio_gameplay_mixer_ready, bus/cue/trigger/command rows, paused/faded buses, looping/spatial commands, render frame/sample counters, sample absolute sums, and sample/payload diagnostics from first-party planner/mixer evidence without native device or codec breadth claims. sample_2d_desktop_runtime_package and sample_generated_desktop_runtime_3d_package emit audio_production_* selected package evidence with device and HRTF host gates still unavailable. Windows MK_audio_wasapi WasapiAudioRuntime/WasapiAudioDevice plans COM startup, opens the default eRender/eConsole shared-mode endpoint through private IAudioClient/IAudioRenderClient implementation, queues silence or rendered interleaved float PCM, reports queued frames, exposes pause/resume/clear controls, and records callback-free lifecycle evidence through wasapi_audio_device_lifecycle_evidence without public COM or WASAPI handles; platform/android contains a private AAudio output adapter that opens a low-latency float32 output stream, uses an atomic FIFO for callback-safe sample transfer, and starts/stops with Android lifecycle events without public AAudio handles; broad codec support, streaming decode integration, HRTF execution parity, broad DSP graph execution, device hotplug/selection parity, mixer authoring UI, middleware parity, and richer codec workflows remain follow-up work

gameCodeGuidance.currentNavigation: NavigationGrid, NavigationGridCoord, NavigationPathRequest, NavigationPathResult, NavigationPathStatus, and find_navigation_path provide a standard-library-only deterministic cardinal-grid A* foundation with walkability, positive traversal costs, stable tie-breaking, invalid/blocked endpoint diagnostics, no-path reporting, total path cost, and visited-node counts. NavigationPoint2, NavigationPointPathBuildResult, build_navigation_point_path_result, build_navigation_point_path, NavigationPathFollowRequest, advance_navigation_path_following, NavigationArriveSteeringRequest, and calculate_navigation_arrive_steering provide Path Following / Steering Foundation v0 for deterministic grid-path-to-point conversion, explicit target cursor advancement, max-step and arrival-radius movement, advanced/remaining distance reporting, and single-agent arrive steering desired velocity/speed. NavigationAgentConfig, NavigationAgentState, NavigationAgentUpdateRequest, NavigationAgentUpdateResult, make_navigation_agent_state, replace_navigation_agent_path, cancel_navigation_agent_move, and update_navigation_agent provide Navigation Agent Controller Foundation v0 for deterministic value-type agent movement state/ticks with idle/moving/reached/cancelled/invalid statuses, speed/delta clamping, final-destination slowing, path replacement, remaining-distance reporting, and desired velocity from actual path movement. NavigationGridPathValidationRequest, NavigationGridPathValidationResult, NavigationGridPathValidationStatus, validate_navigation_grid_path, NavigationGridReplanRequest, NavigationGridReplanResult, NavigationGridReplanStatus, and replan_navigation_grid_path provide Grid Dynamic Obstacle Replan v0 for validating remaining cardinal-grid paths against the current grid, reporting empty/start/goal/out-of-bounds/blocked/non-cardinal/unsupported-adjacency/cost-overflow diagnostics, reusing valid remaining paths, and recalculating deterministic replacement paths around newly blocked cells through find_navigation_path. NavigationLocalAvoidanceRequest, NavigationLocalAvoidanceResult, NavigationLocalAvoidanceDiagnostic, and calculate_navigation_local_avoidance provide Navigation Agent Local Avoidance v0 for finite-input single-agent desired velocity adjustment against explicit nearby neighbor rows, duplicate-neighbor and calculation-overflow diagnostics, deterministic neighbor ordering, closest-approach prediction over the requested time window, overlap/future-collision separation, applied-neighbor counts, and max-speed clamping. NavigationGridPathSmoothingRequest, NavigationGridPathSmoothingResult, NavigationGridPathSmoothingStatus, and smooth_navigation_grid_path provide Navigation Grid Path Smoothing v0 for reducing validated cardinal grid paths through deterministic walkable line-of-sight while preserving start and goal waypoints. NavigationGridAgentPathRequest, NavigationGridAgentPathPlan, NavigationGridAgentPathStatus, NavigationGridAgentPathDiagnostic, and plan_navigation_grid_agent_path provide Navigation Production Path v1 by composing pathfinding, optional smoothing, point-path mapping, diagnostics, point-count/cost evidence, and NavigationAgentState creation for generated-game route setup. NavigationNavmeshPathRequest, NavigationNavmeshPathResult, NavigationNavmeshDynamicObstacle, NavigationNavmeshReadinessConfig, NavigationNavmeshReadinessReport, plan_navigation_navmesh_path, and evaluate_navigation_navmesh_readiness provide Navigation Navmesh Production Path v1 by composing caller-authored scene-ref polygon rows, portal rows, deterministic dynamic-obstacle blocking, point-path mapping, status diagnostics, visited counts, cost evidence, and value-only readiness diagnostics for generated-game smoke proof. NavigationCrowdAgentDesc, NavigationCrowdPlanRequest, NavigationCrowdAgentPlanRow, NavigationCrowdPlanResult, NavigationCrowdPlanStatus, NavigationCrowdPlanDiagnostic, NavigationCrowdReadinessConfig, NavigationCrowdReadinessReport, NavigationCrowdReadinessStatus, NavigationCrowdReadinessDiagnostic, plan_navigation_navmesh_crowd, and evaluate_navigation_crowd_readiness provide Navigation Navmesh Crowd Readiness v1 for value-only deterministic crowd rows over navmesh routes, dynamic-obstacle participation, local-avoidance aggregation, max-agent budget rejection, duplicate/invalid-agent diagnostics, source-order checks, row budgets, readiness summaries, and stable replay counters. Generated games can use these for tile/grid movement, selected scene-ref polygon routing, value-only crowd movement authoring, and headless AI tests. navmesh asset import, spline/funnel smoothing, persistent/full crowd simulation beyond value-only batch planning, animation-aware steering, networked crowd determinism, RVO/ORCA, scene/physics integration, editor visualization, middleware, and background navigation jobs are not implemented yet.
gameCodeGuidance.currentAi: BehaviorTreeDesc, BehaviorTreeNodeDesc, BehaviorTreeNodeKind, BehaviorTreeLeafResult, BehaviorTreeStatus, BehaviorTreeDiagnostic, BehaviorTreeTickResult, BehaviorTreeBlackboardValue, BehaviorTreeBlackboard, BehaviorTreeBlackboardCondition, BehaviorTreeEvaluationContext, and evaluate_behavior_tree provide Behavior Tree Foundation v0 plus Behavior Tree Blackboard Conditions v0 for deterministic memoryless sequence, selector, action, and condition evaluation. BehaviorAuthoringDocument, BehaviorAuthoringBehaviorDesc, BehaviorAuthoringActionBinding, BehaviorAuthoringValidationContext, BehaviorAuthoringValidationResult, BehaviorAuthoringTraceRow, BehaviorAuthoringDiagnostic, validate_behavior_authoring_document, BehaviorAuthoringReadinessConfig, BehaviorAuthoringReadinessReport, and evaluate_behavior_authoring_readiness provide AI Behavior Authoring Foundation v1 for validating reusable generated-game behavior documents and summarizing deterministic trace, action-binding, blackboard-condition, validation-diagnostic, and budget evidence against available blackboard keys and supported game-owned action ids before runtime execution. AiPerceptionAgent2D, AiPerceptionTarget2D, AiPerceptionSnapshot2D, build_ai_perception_snapshot_2d, write_ai_perception_blackboard, AiPerceptionReadinessConfig, AiPerceptionReadinessReport, and evaluate_ai_perception_readiness_2d provide AI Perception Services v1 for explicit caller-supplied 2D sight/hearing classification, stable primary-target selection, duplicate target rejection, finite validation, blackboard projection into caller-selected fact keys, and readiness summaries over stable-primary, blackboard-projection, visible/audible-target, and target-budget evidence. Action leaves are game-owned and supplied as explicit node-id statuses; condition leaves can either use legacy game-supplied leaf statuses or typed blackboard bindings over bool, signed integer, finite double, and string facts. The evaluator, authoring validator, and perception projection own validation, stable traversal/order, status propagation, visited-node traces, behavior-authoring trace rows, and missing-root, duplicate-node-id, missing-child, empty-composite, leaf-has-children, missing-leaf-result, invalid-leaf-result, duplicate-leaf-result, duplicate-blackboard-key, invalid-blackboard-key, write_ai_perception_blackboard, evaluate_behavior_tree, plan_navigation_grid_agent_path, replan_navigation_grid_path, calculate_navigation_local_avoidance, plan_navigation_navmesh_path, evaluate_physics_character_dynamic_policy_3d, build_physics_world_3d_from_authored_collision_scene, move_physics_character_controller_3d, render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float, and MK_animation Memory decay, teams/factions, broad event stimuli, AI performance services, async tasks, decorators/services, utility AI, scene/physics/navigation engine integration, persistent blackboard services, middleware, scripting, and editor graph authoring are not implemented yet.

gameCodeGuidance.currentJobExecutionWorkerPool: MK_core Job Execution Worker Pool v1 adds a real dependency-free first-party worker execution API for generated games, samples, and host adapters that need bounded CPU task execution before broader all-core optimization claims: JobExecutionPool, JobExecutionPoolDesc, JobExecutionBatchDesc, JobExecutionTaskDesc, JobExecutionContext, JobExecutionStopToken, JobExecutionRunResult, JobExecutionPoolStatus, JobExecutionRunStatus, JobExecutionDiagnosticCode, execute(batch), request_stop, stop_and_drain, worker_threads_started, job_execution_pool_status_label, job_execution_run_status_label, and job_execution_diagnostic_code_label. The pool is non-copyable and non-movable, starts explicit worker_count RAII-owned std::thread workers, owns bounded per-worker queues, supplies worker-local ScratchArena through JobExecutionContext, supports JobExecutionStopToken cooperative stop, drains each execute(batch) call before returning, catches task exceptions, and maps deterministic publish order plus queue/scratch/dependency evidence into JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence. It fail-closes invalid configuration, invalid tasks, queue overflow, blocked dependencies, dependency cycles, task exceptions, and stopped-pool execution. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-foundation reports package-visible job_execution_foundation_status=ready, job_execution_foundation_ready=1, job_execution_foundation_pool_status=ready, job_execution_foundation_run_status=ready, job_execution_foundation_worker_threads_started=2, job_execution_foundation_tasks_submitted=3, job_execution_foundation_tasks_executed=3, job_execution_foundation_tasks_failed=0, job_execution_foundation_task_side_effects=3, job_execution_foundation_execution_rows=3, job_execution_foundation_queue_rows=2, job_execution_foundation_deterministic_merges=3, positive job_execution_foundation_scratch_bytes, positive job_execution_foundation_scratch_high_water_bytes, and zero work-stealing/affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side-effect counters. This is real worker-thread execution evidence only; it does not claim work stealing execution, affinity pinning, NUMA placement, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentJobExecutionWorkStealing: MK_core Job Execution Work Stealing v1 adds opt-in work_stealing_enabled execution to JobExecutionPool and enable_work_stealing selection through JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDesc for generated games, samples, and host adapters that need first-party worker-queue stealing without OS affinity or vendor policy. JobExecutionRunResult reports work_stealing_applied, steal_attempt_count, steal_success_count, and worker_wait_count, and execute(batch) injects runtime steal/wait counters into JobSchedulingExecutionEvidence queue rows while preserving deterministic publish order and drain-before-return semantics. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-work-stealing reports package-visible job_execution_work_stealing_status=ready, job_execution_work_stealing_ready=1, job_execution_work_stealing_worker_threads_started=2, job_execution_work_stealing_tasks_submitted=2, job_execution_work_stealing_tasks_executed=2, job_execution_work_stealing_tasks_failed=0, job_execution_work_stealing_task_side_effects=2, job_execution_work_stealing_deterministic_merges=2, positive job_execution_work_stealing_steal_attempts, positive job_execution_work_stealing_steal_successes, positive job_execution_work_stealing_worker_waits, positive scratch byte/high-water counters, job_execution_work_stealing_applied=1, and zero affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side-effect counters. This is narrow first-party work-stealing execution evidence only; it does not claim affinity pinning, NUMA placement execution, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad all-core CPU/GPU/memory optimization readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentJobExecutionTopologyPolicy: MK_core Job Execution Topology Policy v1 adds portable worker-count selection for generated games, samples, and host adapters that need to size a bounded JobExecutionPool without claiming all-core or vendor-specific tuning: JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDesc, JobExecutionTopologyPolicy, JobExecutionTopologyPolicyStatus, JobExecutionTopologyPolicyDiagnosticCode, select_job_execution_topology_policy, observe_job_execution_logical_processor_count, job_execution_topology_policy_status_label, and job_execution_topology_policy_diagnostic_code_label. The policy treats observed logical processor count as explicit evidence, keeps a fallback count for hardware_concurrency=0 or missing evidence, applies requested worker count, worker count limit, and reserved logical processor rules, derives JobExecutionPoolDesc, emits JobWorkerTopologyRow, and reports host_evidence_required for missing processor-group or NUMA evidence. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-topology-policy reports package-visible job_execution_topology_policy_status=ready, job_execution_topology_policy_ready=1, job_execution_topology_policy_observed_logical_processors=8, job_execution_topology_policy_effective_logical_processors=8, job_execution_topology_policy_selected_worker_count=2, job_execution_topology_policy_worker_count_limit=2, job_execution_topology_policy_reserved_logical_processors=1, job_execution_topology_policy_fallback_used=0, job_execution_topology_policy_worker_count_limited_by_cap=1, job_execution_topology_policy_requested_worker_count_used=0, job_execution_topology_policy_clamped_to_logical_processors=0, job_execution_topology_policy_processor_group_count=1, job_execution_topology_policy_numa_node_count=1, and zero processor-group/NUMA/affinity/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL side-effect counters. This is portable worker-count policy evidence only; it does not claim work stealing execution, affinity pinning, NUMA placement execution, hybrid P-core/E-core policy, SMT policy, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor parity, cross-backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization readiness.
gameCodeGuidance.currentJobExecutionPlacementPolicy: MK_core Job Execution Placement Policy v1 provides host-independent CPU placement intent and fail-closed evidence before OS-specific execution: JobExecutionPlacementPolicyMode, JobExecutionPlacementPolicyDesc, JobExecutionPlacementPolicy, select_job_execution_placement_policy, placement status/diagnostic label helpers, inherited JobExecutionPoolDesc rows, NUMA/core-type/SMT evidence fields, and explicit side-effect flags. sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-job-execution-placement-policy reports job_execution_placement_policy_status=ready, job_execution_placement_policy_ready=1, requested/selected mode os_default, job_execution_placement_policy_host_evidence_required_diagnostics=1 on the separate missing-NUMA probe row, inherited two-worker work-stealing evidence, zero performance/efficiency core counts, unknown SMT sibling topology, and zero affinity/NUMA/SIMD/GPU-overlap/CUDA/HIP/SYCL execution counters. Generated games may use this as policy evidence, but must still hand off requests for Linux affinity, NUMA allocation execution, hybrid P-core/E-core pinning, SMT scheduling, SIMD dispatch, GPU async overlap, CUDA/HIP/SYCL paths, cross-vendor/backend parity, or broad CPU/GPU/memory optimization.

Generated games and samples can request selected package evidence with sample_desktop_runtime_game --require-windows-cpu-set-worker-placement, which reports windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_status=ready, windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_ready=1, positive windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_selected_cpu_sets, windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_worker_rows=2, windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_worker_threads_started=2, windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_attempts=2, windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_applied=2, windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_selected_cpu_set_applications=2, windows_cpu_set_worker_placement_native_thread_handles_exposed=0, and zero Linux affinity, NUMA allocation, hybrid/SMT policy, SIMD, GPU async-overlap, CUDA, HIP, and SYCL side-effect counters. gameCodeGuidance.currentRuntimeSimulationPersistence: Generated games can use mirakana::runtime::RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRequest, RuntimeSimulationPersistencePlan, RuntimeSimulationPersistentEntityRow, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceMigrationStep, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceDiagnostic, RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRemediationAction, and plan_runtime_simulation_persistence from MK_runtime after load_runtime_session_profile_documents when selected simulation resume needs stable save-slot, world id, snapshot id, world tick, entity state rows, supported schema migration chains, or corrupt-save remediation evidence. Non-save profile document failures do not block valid save recovery. The planner is value-only, reports RuntimeSimulationPersistenceDiagnosticCode rows for blocking save document status, corrupt save documents, missing save.slot/world.id/snapshot.id/world.tick evidence, malformed entity.<id>.(type|region|state_hash) rows, missing required entities, unsupported schema versions, invalid migration rows, and missing migration-chain steps, and leaves filesystem/package state untouched. RuntimeSimulationPersistenceRemediationAction is a recommendation only. This is not a cloud save system, cross-device sync, multiplayer replication layer, binary compatibility policy, platform user-directory resolver, migration executor, filesystem/package mutation helper, native handle surface, or game-specific component serializer beyond reviewed save keys.
